data.title: Liberland Fires Technology Secretary After Allegations of Blockchain Control Centralization
data.description:
Liberland has fired its secretary of technology, Dorian Stern Vukotic, after alleging he centralized control of the Liberland Blockchain and blocked President Vit Jedlicka from voting.

According to NS3.AI, Liberland also demanded that Vukotic return Ministry of Finance funds and transfer control of a liquidity pool within seven days.

The report did not provide additional details on the alleged centralization or the amount of funds involved.

data.title: James Chanos: SpaceX Mega IPO Is a Troubling Sign for Markets
data.description: Veteran short seller James Chanos said a SpaceX mega IPO is a troubling sign for broader markets. According to Bloomberg, Chanos said “we’re going to shatter” records this year when it comes to IPOs, speaking on “Bloomberg Money” with Tom Keene and Scarlet Fu.

data.title: Japan Lower House Passes Bill to Bring Crypto Under FIEA, Opening Path to Spot ETFs
data.description:
Japan’s lower house passed a bill that would move cryptocurrencies under the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act, creating a pathway for spot crypto ETFs and introducing a flat 20% capital-gains tax.

According to NS3.AI, the reform is not yet law because the bill must still pass the upper house, where approval is widely expected.

Representatives from the Tokyo Stock Exchange said crypto ETFs could begin listing as early as 2027 once the regulatory framework is finalized.

The flat 20% capital-gains tax is expected to take effect in 2028 under Japan’s 2026 Tax Reform Outline.

data.title: Misam Abidi Indicted Over Alleged Star Credit Holdings Crypto Ponzi Scheme
data.description:
A federal indictment accuses Misam M. Abidi of running an alleged crypto Ponzi scheme through Star Credit Holdings from 2020–2024 and diverting more than $1.9 million to himself and family.

According to NS3.AI, Abidi faces 11 counts tied to the alleged scheme.

The charges include wire fraud, money laundering, operating an unlicensed money transmitting business, and filing false tax returns.

data.title: Columbia to Reinstate SAT, ACT Requirement for Admissions Starting in 2027
data.description: Columbia University will again require applicants to submit SAT or ACT scores for admission starting in 2027. According to Bloomberg, the university said a faculty review found the tests were “a useful indicator of potential student success.”

data.title: World Cup Betting Volume Could Reach $10 Billion, Bernstein Analysts Say
data.description:
The World Cup could generate up to $10 billion in betting volume across sports-betting and prediction-market platforms. According to NS3.AI, Bernstein analysts expect roughly $3 billion in additional wagers tied to the tournament.

The analysts also estimate there will be about 60% more bettable inventory than in prior tournaments, suggesting an expanded range of markets and events available for wagering.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Putin Says Russia Expanding Domestic Satellite Constellation, Vows More Strikes on Ukraine Infrastructure
data.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Russia is expanding a constellation of domestically produced satellites and vowed to step up strikes on Ukrainian infrastructure. According to Bloomberg, he linked the satellite expansion to broader efforts as the war continues.

data.title: Robinhood Says Platform Traffic Hit a Record High
data.description:
Robinhood said its platform traffic hit an all-time high today.

According to Jin10, the company described the traffic level as the highest in its history.

data.title: STOCKS | Goldman Sachs And Morgan Stanley Expected To Earn About $100 Million Each From SpaceX IPO Fees
data.description:
Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley are expected to each receive about $100 million in underwriting fees from SpaceX’s initial public offering.

According to Jin10, The Wall Street Journal reported the estimates for the two banks’ potential fee income from the deal.

data.title: Pakistan Prime Minister Says Final Agreed Text of Peace Deal Has Been Reached
data.description:
Pakistan’s prime minister said a final agreed text of a peace agreement has been reached and that Pakistan is working closely with both sides to finalize arrangements for the next stage.

According to Odaily, the prime minister wrote on social media that Pakistan has been conducting intensive mediation efforts while facing what he described as a continuous disinformation campaign by forces seeking to undermine the peace agreement.

He said Pakistan is cooperating closely with both parties to complete the next phase of planning and urged ignoring what he called disruptive noise.

data.title: Nasdaq Turns Higher, Up 0.2%
data.description:
The Nasdaq turned higher, rising 0.2%.

According to Jin10, the index was last up 0.2% after moving into positive territory.

data.title: Stock jumps above $160 after IPO priced at $135
data.description: Shares traded above $160 in volatile action after last night’s IPO priced at $135, according to CoinDesk.

data.title: SpaceX IPO Tokenized Asset SPCXon Lists on Ondo Global Markets on Debut Day
data.description:
Ondo Finance said it listed the tokenized asset SPCXon on Ondo Global Markets on the first day of SpaceX’s IPO. According to Odaily, Ondo Finance posted the update on X.

Ondo Finance described the IPO as the largest in history. It said SPCXon supports Solana, Ethereum, and BNB Chain.

data.title: CFRA Initiates Coverage of SpaceX With Sell Rating and $115 Target Price
data.description: CFRA has initiated coverage of SpaceX (SPCX.O) with a sell rating and a target price of $115. According to Odaily, the research firm issued the rating and price target in its first coverage of the company.

data.title: JBS NV to Close Pennsylvania Beef Plant Amid US Cattle Shortage
data.description: JBS NV is closing a beef plant in Pennsylvania amid a severe US cattle shortage that is pressuring meatpackers. according to Bloomberg,

data.title: VanEck touts BNB usage after launching spot BNB ETF VBNB on Nasdaq
data.description: VanEck is betting BNB’s real-world activity and revenue generation can differentiate it in a crowded crypto ETF market after launching the first U.S. spot BNB ETF, VBNB, on Nasdaq. In its second sentence, according to CoinDesk, VanEck Director of Digital Assets Product Kyle DaCruz said the firm targets blockchains with measurable adoption, and the ETF has attracted roughly $2 million in assets since launch. DaCruz cited BNB Chain metrics including 33 million monthly active users, 2.1 million daily active users, about $100 billion in monthly stablecoin transfer volume and $16 billion in stablecoins minted, and said BNB generates roughly $160 million in annual revenue.

data.title: Binance Says SpaceX-Linked SPCXx IPO Campaign Is Canceled, Refunds Completed and SPCXB Airdrop Set
data.description:
Binance announced on X that its Binance Wallet SPCXx IPO Campaign will not continue due to force majeure factors. The company said all participating users have already received full refunds of their USDC, returned to their Binance Wallet addresses. Binance also said all participating users will equally share an airdrop valued at $1,000,000 in bStocks SpaceX tokens (SPCXB), which will be automatically distributed to their Binance spot accounts.

Section: Trading Access

Binance said users can now submit limit orders during regular trading hours for whole-share trading of SpaceX (SPCX) stock. The update follows the cancellation of the SPCXx IPO Campaign and is presented as an alternative way for users to place orders related to SpaceX (SPCX) within Binance’s offering. The announcement did not provide additional details on timing beyond stating that the order submission feature is available now, nor did it specify further conditions related to the airdrop distribution beyond equal sharing among participating users and automatic delivery to spot accounts.

data.title: STOCKS | Bernstein Reiterates Buy Rating on Amazon and Sets $315 Price Target
data.description:
Bernstein reiterated its buy rating for Amazon stock on June 10 and set a $315 price target.

According to NS3.AI, Amazon opened Friday at $241 after rising 1.47% on Thursday.

data.title: Binance to List SpaceX bStocks (SPCXB) Spot Pair and Add Algo Trading Bots
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the exchange will open spot trading and enable Spot Algo Trading Bots services for the bStocks SpaceX (SPCXB) trading pair. The listing is scheduled for 2026-06-12 17:00 (UTC) with the spot pair SPCXB/USDT. Binance also said it will apply zero maker fees to the SPCXB/USDT pair during a defined promotion window.

Section: Fees and Timeline

Binance stated that the zero maker fee schedule for SPCXB/USDT will run from the listing time* through 2026-08-31 23:59 (UTC). Section: Deposits, Withdrawals, and Token Details

Binance said deposits and withdrawals for SPCXB will open at 2026-06-13 13:30 (UTC). The announcement listed the smart contract information on BNB Smart Chain for SPCXB as 0xbe9D156892E55e7154BcD3cB0FEA677F9D3103E1. Section: Product Description

Binance described bStocks as tokenized securities issued by BTech Holdings Limited, a Binance group affiliate, and classified as Certificates representing certain Financial Instruments (paragraph 92, Schedule 1 to FSMR). Binance said bStocks represent an interest in underlying securities held by the issuer and do not provide direct ownership of the underlying shares.

data.title: Kalshi’s crypto perpetuals spark debate over whether they’re futures or swaps
data.description: Kalshi’s launch of CFTC-regulated crypto perpetuals has reignited debate over whether the contracts should be classified as futures or swaps. John Lothian, publisher of John Lothian News, said perpetuals resemble swaps because funding-rate mechanisms create recurring bilateral cash-flow payments, while Kalshi head of exchange analytics Udesh Jha argued they function like futures because they are exchange-traded, centrally cleared and track spot markets, according to CoinDesk. The classification could affect access, retail participation, tax treatment and oversight, with both sides also disputing manipulation risks tied to funding-rate calculations.
